results.title: Welcome, TripCase Travelers | TripIt
results.content:
With TripIt and TripIt Pro, you’ll find many of the features you’ve come to know and love from TripCase, as well as an array of useful tools that set TripIt apart. As a long-standing innovator in travel organization, TripIt’s platform is continually evolving, introducing new features designed to make your travel experiences smoother and more enjoyable. From comprehensive itinerary management to real-time flight alerts, and so much more, TripIt is your ideal TripCase alternative.

Get Started with TripIt     Sign Me Up for Pro

TripIt is 20 this year! Explore two decades of memories and milestones with us.

Discover More

# Welcome, TripCase Travelers

We understand that the news of TripCase shutting down on April 1, 2025 may have left you searching for a reliable alternative to manage your travels. TripIt is the ultimate itinerary management app, helping travelers organize their bookings, reservations, and plans in one place for seamless travel experiences. [...] ## Frequently Asked Questions

### What is the difference between TripIt and TripCase?

TripIt and TripCase are both travel organization tools, and while TripCase was powerful, TripIt offers a more seamless user experience with additional features like automatic itinerary creation and broader integration with travel services. To see TripIt in action and learn more about its unique benefits, check out our video here.

### Can I transfer existing trips from TripCase to TripIt?

You can download PDFs of your plans by using the Print Itinerary option within TripCase.  You can then forward your PDFs to plans@tripit.com.

results.title: TripIt: The Best Travel App (To Replace TripCase) – A Purple Life
results.content:
## Conclusion

So that was my experience trying to find the best replacement for the travel organization app TripCase. It was a great 12 years, but it’s time to move on and I’m hopeful that TripIt will help me keep my plans organized as I continue my full-time nomad lifestyle.

### Share this:

### Like this:

### Related

## Post navigation

## 12 thoughts on “TripIt: The Best Travel App (To Replace TripCase)”

Hey, Purple – hope all is well!

I was also a TripCase user for a while but switched to Kayak about a decade ago and haven’t looked back. Yes, that’s the same Kayak used for searching for flights and hotels. [...] I forgot about TripCase till I read it here. Thanks for doing the research for an alternative. I’ll make a plan while saving for my first trip in Mexico City and Amsterdam.

I used Flighty for flights. Like TripCase it notifies me of flight and gate changes / updates.

Of course 🙂 . And that’s really cool about Flighty – I haven’t heard of that before and these new apps seem to only alert you if you have their Pro version so I’ll definitely check it out.

This is EXACTLY the info I came here for! Sad about trip case. Looking for the next app. Gonna revisit Tripit again too! Thank you for all the details!

I’m so happy to hear that it was helpful 🙂 ! [...] For example, years ago my Mom and I were flying to Fiji and the airline ended up delaying our flight 12 hours. The first time I heard about the delay was from TripCase – not the airline. That heads up allowed us to plan accordingly and I’ll never forget it.

Especially for something like my European itinerary to visit my friends in my 20s where I only stayed in each city 2-4 days, knowing what was up in an organized fashion was necessary and TripCase allowed me to do that years ago and still did to this day.

### PROs

These were the pros of TripCase in my mind:

Plan and document manual test cases and test executions

## 5. Testpad

Testpad is an alternative to old-school test case management tools or spreadsheets. It helps you find bugs that matter faster so you can spend more time testing. It combines the simplicity of checklists and the power of spreadsheet tools. [...] Best Test Management Tools (New 2025)

## 1. Testmo

Type: Modern test management with Jira add-on

Testmo is the top unified test management tools. It supports managing manual test cases, exploratory testing and test automation — all in one platform. It also comes with full integration with issue tracking tools, CI pipelines and any automation tool. 🎬 Watch the Testmo test management demo video. [...] Manage manual and automated tests as Jira issues, customize screens, fields and workflows
 Create test plans for tracking a set of tests and planned or ad hoc test executions
 Define parameters to run multiple tests against
 View the test coverage of requirements with interactive charts
 Link to other Jira issues (like user stories, epics, and defects) for full traceability in Jira

Go to Xray

## 3. TestRail

TestRail is one of the most widely-used test management tools in the industry. It allows you to centralize all of your test cases, test plans, and test runs, and supports manual and automated testing. It is known for its extensive API methods and customizability, as well as scalability for large enterprise teams.

results.title: Top 7+ Free Test Management Tools for 2026 - Celoxis®
results.content:
For teams researching free test management tools in 2026, BugBug fits best when the primary need is automated web testing with minimal setup. It allows users to record, edit, and execute browser tests directly from a simple interface without requiring deep programming knowledge.

BugBug supports modern web applications and enables teams to validate user flows, regression paths, and UI behavior across builds. However, it is not a full test case management system like Tuskr or Qase. It works better as a test automation layer rather than a centralized QA management platform.

#### Key Features

#### Pros

#### Cons

#### Free Plan / Trial Details

BugBug offers a free trial with limited usage.

Ongoing access requires a paid subscription based on usage and features.

#### Best Suited For [...] BlazeMeter is a continuous testing platform focused primarily on performance testing, load testing, and API testing. It supports tools like JMeter, Selenium, and Gatling, allowing teams to run large-scale performance tests across cloud environments.

For teams searching for free test management tools in 2026, BlazeMeter is not a traditional test case management system. Instead, it is a performance testing and continuous testing platform designed for DevOps and engineering teams that need to validate system stability under load.

BlazeMeter helps organizations simulate real-world traffic, detect bottlenecks, and compare performance metrics across builds. It is often used in CI/CD pipelines to ensure application reliability before production releases.

#### Key Features

#### Pros [...] #### Key Features

#### Pros

#### Cons

#### Free Plan / Trial Details

BlazeMeter offers a limited free tier for small-scale testing.

Advanced performance and enterprise features require paid plans.

#### Best Suited For

### 11. Loadium – Best for Cloud-Based Performance & Load Testing Platform

Loadium is a cloud-based performance testing platform designed to help teams validate application scalability, reliability, and speed under real-world load conditions. While not a traditional test case management tool, Loadium supports performance testing workflows and is often evaluated by QA teams looking for free or trial-based load testing solutions.

results.title: What is best test case management tool you have used? - Discussions
results.content:
The reports didn’t include the fact, that some test cases were executed more than once. That was great for the overall coverage, but sometimes, we wanted an overview, how many testcase executions were done alltogether for a project, and it was really difficult to get that number.

TestLink is opensource, but it seems that it’s no longer developed and maintained. :frowning:

:frowning:

We’ve been looking for alternatives, and among others we came across Klaros and TestMonitor, which seemed quite useful. But except from a few experiments with their free trials, I don’t have any practical experiences. [...] It doesn’t have as many features as TestRail, but it is very stable, and what I’ve found most helpful is the issue-tracking integrations, which work with many different bug trackers. (and will automate the entering of issue tracker ID’s)

They have the ability to create and associate requirements with Test cases; test steps can be numbered using test formatting within a test case, which are then stored in test suites. Test runs will then allow you to document and record your test executions.

I’m not sure about your additional feature list, but your must-haves are similar to what I use, so it would certainly be a contender if you are looking for a less complex tool.

@captainjonesy2 you should try testomat.io , it covers all your needs. [...] Chris

In my previous job we used TestLink.

You can manage requirements and test cases, both can be organised in folders or test suites.  
TestLink automatically assigns a unique intern number to test cases, which can be used for cross references and for the requirement coverage. It has great options for reporting, but I’m not sure about generating reports based on custom fields.  
Test cases can have a status, like draft, or final.  
You can have a separate test plan for the regression testing and then create reports from this test plan.
